decks.notes: The deck revolves around assembling and executing the Doomsday combo to win the game in a single turn. Cards like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Thoughtseize help set up the combo pieces needed, while cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual provide mana acceleration. Thassa's Oracle is used as the win condition once Doomsday is resolved, and protection is provided by cards like Force of Will and Flusterstorm. The deck aims to quickly and efficiently combo off before the opponent can disrupt the game plan.

The focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of artifacts that lock down the opponent's options while ramping up mana production. By utilizing powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Karn, the Great Creator, as well as disruption tools like Trinisphere and Pithing Needle, the deck aims to create a prison-like environment for the opponent. Additionally, the inclusion of Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms provides powerful finishers to close out the game once the control aspect is established.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures, combined with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck uses a combination of powerful creatures like Guide of Souls and Amped Raptor, along with combat tricks like Ocelot Pride to quickly close out the game. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide additional reach and removal options, while Static Prison helps control the opponent's board. This deck aims to apply constant pressure and maintain tempo throughout the game.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure through efficient, aggressive creatures. By combining powerful disruption spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize with cheap removal like Fatal Push and Snuff Out, the deck can control the game tempo and protect its threats. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and helps smooth out draws, while Barrowgoyf and Orcish Bowmasters offer consistent damage output. With a mix of counterspells, removal, and card filtering, the deck aims to outpace the opponent and secure victories through tempo advantage.

This deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures and disruptive spells to control the pace of the game and maintain pressure on the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent with powerful disruption like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Force of Will, the deck seeks to dictate the flow of the game and keep the opponent on the back foot. Additionally, cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Daze provide card advantage and tempo plays to further solidify the deck's aggressive strategy. Overall, the deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creatures, then bringing them back into play through reanimation spells such as Reanimate and Animate Dead. This allows the player to cheat expensive creatures like Griselbrand and Ashen Rider into play early in the game, overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats. The use of cards like Thoughtseize and Unmask helps protect the combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to win the game through powerful creatures and efficient graveyard manipulation.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier using cards like Sneak Attack and Show and Tell. By utilizing fast mana accelerants like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with big threats early in the game. With access to counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation for protection, the deck also includes some control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck focuses on executing a powerful combo strategy to win the game.

The deck focuses on establishing a lock by using artifacts to control the opponent's resources and disrupt their game plan. It utilizes cards like Urza's Saga, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere to slow down the opponent while generating value through cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. The goal is to create a prison-like board state where the opponent is unable to effectively play their spells, ultimately leading to victory through attrition or by utilizing powerful finishers like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.

The main concept of the deck is to quickly and efficiently bring powerful creatures from the graveyard onto the battlefield, using cards like Reanimate, Shallow Grave, and Animate Dead. The deck utilizes cards like Faithless Looting and Thoughtseize to set up and protect the combo. With efficient mana acceleration from cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to cheat out creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty early in the game to overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of disruptive cards like Unmask and Cabal Therapy helps to control the opponent's game plan while the combo is being set up.

The core strategy of this deck is to set up and execute the Doomsday combo, using cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to quickly generate mana. The deck includes disruption and card selection through Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Brainstorm, while also featuring control elements with cards like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The win condition is typically using Doomsday to set up a winning sequence of cards, such as Thassa's Oracle, to secure victory in the late game. The deck aims to efficiently assemble its combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a quick and aggressive tempo. Utilizing efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Daze to control the board, combined with disruptive cards like Thoughtseize, the deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot. The inclusion of powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares ensures that the deck can close out games quickly once it has gained control. The deck's mana base is carefully constructed to support its game plan while also providing flexibility in casting spells.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive Eldrazi creatures onto the battlefield while disrupting opponents with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void. The inclusion of cards like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal allows for explosive starts, while Cavern of Souls ensures that the Eldrazi spells cannot be countered. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with large threats like Reality Smasher and Sire of Seven Deaths, closing out the game before opponents have a chance to stabilize.

The deck is focused on assembling the Cephalid Breakfast combo, using Cephalid Illusionist and Nomads en-Kor to mill the library and win the game with Thassa's Oracle. The deck includes disruption with cards like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to protect the combo pieces. It also includes card draw and selection with Brainstorm and Ponder to help find the combo pieces quickly. The deck aims to win the game quickly and efficiently through its combo strategy, while also having some interaction and protection against opposing strategies.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through land manipulation and disruption, utilizing cards like Urza's Saga, Exploration, and Crop Rotation to generate advantage. The deck aims to lock down the opponent's resources with cards like The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale and Wasteland, while also threatening a quick win condition with cards like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. By efficiently managing lands and employing control elements, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through resource denial and strategic plays.

The key concept of this deck is to maintain control of the board with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells while also pressuring the opponent with tempo plays. The deck aims to quickly deploy threats like Murktide Regent and Cori-Steel Cutter while protecting them with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. The deck's mana base is designed to consistently hit its colors and disrupt the opponent's mana base with cards like Wasteland. Overall, the deck looks to apply pressure early and maintain momentum throughout the game.

This deck focuses on an aggressive playstyle while utilizing the Esper colors to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of Yorion as a companion allows for additional value and a longer-term strategy. The deck includes a mix of powerful creatures, disruptive spells, and versatile answers to ensure constant pressure on the opponent. By utilizing cards like Aether Vial and Force of Will, the deck aims to maintain tempo and outmaneuver the opponent, while maintaining card advantage and board presence with cards like Recruiter of the Guard and Skyclave Apparition.
